content.text:
Cách an toàn nhất là **fetch upstream rồi merge** vào branch hiện tại:

```bash
git fetch upstream
git merge upstream/main    # merge vào branch hiện tại
```

Bạn muốn mình chạy luôn không? Hiện tại đang ở branch `develop` — bạn muốn merge upstream/main vào `develop` hay vào `main` trước?

content.text:
Theo config trong project, câu lệnh chạy với config file custom:

```bash
OPENACP_CONFIG_PATH=~/.openacp/config2.json pnpm start
```

Hoặc nếu dùng node trực tiếp:

```bash
OPENACP_CONFIG_PATH=~/.openacp/config2.json node packages/core/dist/cli.js
```

content.text:
Có 2 vấn đề:

1. **ETIMEDOUT** — Không kết nối được tới `api.telegram.org`. Có thể do mạng hoặc cần VPN/proxy.

2. **Dùng `node-fetch` thay vì native fetch** — Stack trace cho thấy request đi qua `node-fetch@2.7.0`, đây là nguyên nhân gây ETIMEDOUT trên Node 20+. Theo CLAUDE.md cũ, grammY cần dùng native fetch: `{ client: { fetch } }`.

Để mình kiểm tra code adapter hiện tại có set native fetch chưa.

content.text:
Vậy lỗi là do **mạng** — không kết nối được tới `api.telegram.org`. Bạn cần bật VPN hoặc proxy để truy cập Telegram API.

Nếu đang dùng proxy, có thể set biến môi trường:

```bash
HTTPS_PROXY=http://127.0.0.1:7890 pnpm start
```

(thay port `7890` bằng port proxy của bạn)

content.text: Vẫn lỗi. `curl` OK nhưng Node.js thì không — có thể do **proxy/VPN chỉ áp dụng cho system nhưng Node.js không dùng system proxy**. Thử kiểm tra:

content.text:
Không có proxy nào được set. `curl` hoạt động nhưng Node.js thì không — rất có thể mạng hiện tại **block kết nối TCP trực tiếp tới Telegram** ở tầng firewall, còn `curl` có thể dùng route khác.

Bạn thử **bật lại VPN** rồi chạy lại xem. Hoặc nếu biết port proxy VPN đang dùng, set:

```bash
HTTPS_PROXY=http://127.0.0.1:<port> OPENACP_CONFIG_PATH=~/.openacp/config2.json pnpm start
```

content.text:
`claude-agent-acp` chưa được cài. Đây là ACP wrapper cho Claude Code.

Bạn có thể cài bằng:

```bash
npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-acp
```

Hoặc nếu chưa có package đó, đổi config để dùng `claude` trực tiếp:

```json
"claude": {
  "command": "claude",
  "args": ["--acp"],
  "env": {}
}
```

Bạn muốn cài package hay đổi config?

content.content:
Usage: claude [options] [command] [prompt]

Claude Code - starts an interactive session by default, use -p/--print for
non-interactive output

Arguments:
  prompt                                            Your prompt

Options:
  --add-dir <directories...>                        Additional directories to allow tool access to
  --agent <agent>                                   Agent for the current session. Overrides the 'agent' setting.
  --agents <json>                                   JSON object defining custom agents (e.g. '{"reviewer": {"description": "Reviews code", "prompt": "You are a code reviewer"}}')
  --allow-dangerously-skip-permissions              Enable bypassing all permission checks as an option, without it being enabled by default. Recommended only for sandboxes with no internet access.
  --allowedTools, --allowed-tools <tools...>        Comma or space-separated list of tool names to allow (e.g. "Bash(git:*) Edit")
  --append-system-prompt <prompt>                   Append a system prompt to the default system prompt
  --betas <betas...>                                Beta headers to include in API requests (API key users only)
  --brief                                           Enable SendUserMessage tool for agent-to-user communication
  --chrome                                          Enable Claude in Chrome integration
  -c, --continue                                    Continue the most recent conversation in the current directory
  --dangerously-skip-permissions                    Bypass all permission checks. Recommended only for sandboxes with no internet access.
  -d, --debug [filter]                              Enable debug mode with optional category filtering (e.g., "api,hooks" or "!1p,!file")
  --debug-file <path>                               Write debug logs to a specific file path (implicitly enables debug mode)
  --disable-slash-commands                          Disable all skills
  --disallowedTools, --disallowed-tools <tools...>  Comma or space-separated list of tool names to deny (e.g. "Bash(git:*) Edit")
  --effort <level>                                  Effort level for the current session (low, medium, high, max)
  --fallback-model <model>                          Enable automatic fallback to specified model when default model is overloaded (only works with --print)
  --file <specs...>                                 File resources to download at startup. Format: file_id:relative_path (e.g., --file file_abc:doc.txt file_def:img.png)
  --fork-session                                    When resuming, create a new session ID instead of reusing the original (use with --resume or --continue)
  --from-pr [value]                                 Resume a session linked to a PR by PR number/URL, or open interactive picker with optional search term
  -h, --help                                        Display help for command

content.text:
Rõ rồi. Trong `setup.ts:54`, thứ tự detect là: `claude-agent-acp` → `claude-code` → `claude`. Setup đã fallback sang `claude-agent-acp` mặc dù không tồn tại, vì nó chỉ ghi default khi không detect được agent nào.

Trên máy bạn có `claude` ở `/Users/kienduong/.local/bin/claude`. Cần check xem `claude` có hỗ trợ ACP không:

content.content: 2.1.80 (Claude Code)

content.text:
Claude Code 2.1.80 chưa có built-in ACP server mode. Package `claude-agent-acp` là một wrapper riêng mà upstream OpenACP mong đợi nhưng chưa được publish.

Hiện tại không có binary nào trên máy hỗ trợ ACP cho Claude. Config2.json không thể fix bằng cách đổi command — vì `claude` CLI không có `--acp` flag.

Bạn muốn:
1. Tạm bỏ agent `claude` và chỉ giữ `codex` (nếu codex có `--acp`)?
2. Hay tìm cách cài `claude-agent-acp`?

content.text:
Lần này không còn lỗi `claude-agent-acp ENOENT` nữa — agent đã resolve được. Nhưng vẫn **ETIMEDOUT** khi kết nối Telegram API (`setMyCommands`).

Lỗi `setMyCommands` là call đầu tiên trong `start()` nên crash trước khi tới phần spawn agent. Vấn đề là mạng — bạn cần bật VPN để kết nối tới `api.telegram.org`.

content.text:
`pnpm dev` chỉ watch TypeScript compile (`tsc --watch`), không tự restart server.

Bạn cần thêm `tsx --watch` hoặc `nodemon` để auto-reload. Cách đơn giản nhất — chạy 2 terminal:

**Terminal 1:** `pnpm dev` (tsc watch)
**Terminal 2:** `node --watch dist/cli.js` (Node 20 có built-in `--watch`)

Hoặc gộp thành 1 script, bạn muốn mình thêm script `dev:start` vào package.json không? Ví dụ:

```bash
OPENACP_CONFIG_PATH=~/.openacp/config2.json node --watch dist/cli.js
```

content.text:
Chạy bằng:

```bash
OPENACP_CONFIG_PATH=~/.openacp/config2.json pnpm dev:start
```

Script sẽ build trước (`tsc`), rồi chạy server với `--watch` — khi file trong `dist/` thay đổi (do `tsc --watch` ở terminal khác hoặc manual build), Node sẽ tự restart.

**Lưu ý:** `node --watch` chỉ watch file JS trong `dist/`. Bạn vẫn cần chạy `pnpm dev` (tsc --watch) ở terminal khác để auto-compile TypeScript → JS.

content.text:
Tìm ra rồi! Kết quả:

| Test | Kết quả |
|------|---------|
| `curl` → Telegram | OK |
| Node TCP → 149.154.166.110:443 | OK |
| Node HTTPS → IP trực tiếp | OK |
| Node `fetch('https://api.telegram.org/')` | **FAILED** |
| Node `https.get` với hostname | **FAILED** |

Vấn đề là **DNS resolution trong Node.js undici/fetch** — khi dùng hostname `api.telegram.org`, undici resolve ra IP nhưng kết nối bị timeout. Có thể do undici thử IPv6 trước (`2001:67c:4e8:f004::9`) và bị block, rồi timeout trước khi fallback IPv4.

content.text:
**Root cause:** Mạng không hỗ trợ IPv6 (`EHOSTUNREACH`). Node.js undici (native fetch) thử IPv6 trước → timeout → không fallback kịp sang IPv4.

Node 20.18.3 có `--dns-result-order=ipv4first` nhưng undici không tôn trọng flag đó. Cần cập nhật Node hoặc force IPv4 trong code. Cách đơn giản nhất — thêm vào đầu `cli.ts` hoặc `main.ts`:

content.text:
Đã fix lại `{ client: { fetch } }`. Tổng hợp flow `start()`:

1. **Dòng 63**: `new Bot(token, { client: { fetch } })` — tạo bot với native fetch
2. **Dòng 88**: `setMyCommands(STATIC_COMMANDS)` — đăng ký 7 commands (new, new_chat, cancel, status, agents, help, menu)
3. Sau đó middleware, topics, handlers, rồi `bot.start()`

`setMyCommands` là call đầu tiên tới Telegram API — nếu mạng lỗi thì crash ngay tại đây. Code đã đúng, build lại rồi chạy thử khi mạng ổn.

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/kienduong/.claude/plugins/cache/claude-plugins-official/superpowers/5.0.5/skills/brainstorming

# Brainstorming Ideas Into Designs

Help turn ideas into fully formed designs and specs through natural collaborative dialogue.

Start by understanding the current project context, then ask questions one at a time to refine the idea. Once you understand what you're building, present the design and get user approval.

<HARD-GATE>
Do NOT invoke any implementation skill, write any code, scaffold any project, or take any implementation action until you have presented a design and the user has approved it. This applies to EVERY project regardless of perceived simplicity.
</HARD-GATE>

## Anti-Pattern: "This Is Too Simple To Need A Design"

Every project goes through this process. A todo list, a single-function utility, a config change — all of them. "Simple" projects are where unexamined assumptions cause the most wasted work. The design can be short (a few sentences for truly simple projects), but you MUST present it and get approval.

## Checklist

You MUST create a task for each of these items and complete them in order:

1. **Explore project context** — check files, docs, recent commits
2. **Offer visual companion** (if topic will involve visual questions) — this is its own message, not combined with a clarifying question. See the Visual Companion section below.
3. **Ask clarifying questions** — one at a time, understand purpose/constraints/success criteria
4. **Propose 2-3 approaches** — with trade-offs and your recommendation
5. **Present design** — in sections scaled to their complexity, get user approval after each section
6. **Write design doc** — save to `docs/superpowers/specs/YYYY-MM-DD-<topic>-design.md` and commit
7. **Spec review loop** — dispatch spec-document-reviewer subagent with precisely crafted review context (never your session history); fix issues and re-dispatch until approved (max 3 iterations, then surface to human)
8. **User reviews written spec** — ask user to review the spec file before proceeding
9. **Transition to implementation** — invoke writing-plans skill to create implementation plan

## Process Flow

```dot
digraph brainstorming {
    "Explore project context" [shape=box];
    "Visual questions ahead?" [shape=diamond];
    "Offer Visual Companion\n(own message, no other content)" [shape=box];
    "Ask clarifying questions" [shape=box];
    "Propose 2-3 approaches" [shape=box];
    "Present design sections" [shape=box];
    "User approves design?" [shape=diamond];
    "Write design doc" [shape=box];
    "Spec review loop" [shape=box];
    "Spec review passed?" [shape=diamond];
    "User reviews spec?" [shape=diamond];
    "Invoke writing-plans skill" [shape=doublecircle];

    "Explore project context" -> "Visual questions ahead?";
    "Visual questions ahead?" -> "Offer Visual Companion\n(own message, no other content)" [label="yes"];
    "Visual questions ahead?" -> "Ask clarifying questions" [label="no"];
    "Offer Visual Companion\n(own message, no other content)" -> "Ask clarifying questions";
    "Ask clarifying questions" -> "Propose 2-3 approaches";
    "Propose 2-3 approaches" -> "Present design sections";
    "Present design sections" -> "User approves design?";
    "User approves design?" -> "Present design sections" [label="no, revise"];
    "User approves design?" -> "Write design doc" [label="yes"];
    "Write design doc" -> "Spec review loop";
    "Spec review loop" -> "Spec review passed?";
    "Spec review passed?" -> "Spec review loop" [label="issues found,\nfix and re-dispatch"];
    "Spec review passed?" -> "User reviews spec?" [label="approved"];
    "User reviews spec?" -> "Write design doc" [label="changes requested"];
    "User reviews spec?" -> "Invoke writing-plans skill" [label="approved"];
}
```

**The terminal state is invoking writing-plans.** Do NOT invoke frontend-design, mcp-builder, or any other implementation skill. The ONLY skill you invoke after brainstorming is writing-plans.

## The Process

**Understanding the idea:**

- Check out the current project state first (files, docs, recent commits)
- Before asking detailed questions, assess scope: if the request describes multiple independent subsystems (e.g., "build a platform with chat, file storage, billing, and analytics"), flag this immediately. Don't spend questions refining details of a project that needs to be decomposed first.
- If the project is too large for a single spec, help the user decompose into sub-projects: what are the independent pieces, how do they relate, what order should they be built? Then brainstorm the first sub-project through the normal design flow. Each sub-project gets its own spec → plan → implementation cycle.
- For appropriately-scoped projects, ask questions one at a time to refine the idea
- Prefer multiple choice questions when possible, but open-ended is fine too
- Only one question per message - if a topic needs more exploration, break it into multiple questions
- Focus on understanding: purpose, constraints, success criteria

**Exploring approaches:**

- Propose 2-3 different approaches with trade-offs
- Present options conversationally with your recommendation and reasoning
- Lead with your recommended option and explain why

**Presenting the design:**

- Once you believe you understand what you're building, present the design
- Scale each section to its complexity: a few sentences if straightforward, up to 200-300 words if nuanced
- Ask after each section whether it looks right so far
- Cover: architecture, components, data flow, error handling, testing
- Be ready to go back and clarify if something doesn't make sense

**Design for isolation and clarity:**

- Break the system into smaller units that each have one clear purpose, communicate through well-defined interfaces, and can be understood and tested independently
- For each unit, you should be able to answer: what does it do, how do you use it, and what does it depend on?
- Can someone understand what a unit does without reading its internals? Can you change the internals without breaking consumers? If not, the boundaries need work.
- Smaller, well-bounded units are also easier for you to work with - you reason better about code you can hold in context at once, and your edits are more reliable when files are focused. When a file grows large, that's often a signal that it's doing too much.

**Working in existing codebases:**

- Explore the current structure before proposing changes. Follow existing patterns.
- Where existing code has problems that affect the work (e.g., a file that's grown too large, unclear boundaries, tangled responsibilities), include targeted improvements as part of the design - the way a good developer improves code they're working in.
- Don't propose unrelated refactoring. Stay focused on what serves the current goal.

## After the Design

**Documentation:**

- Write the validated design (spec) to `docs/superpowers/specs/YYYY-MM-DD-<topic>-design.md`
  - (User preferences for spec location override this default)
- Use elements-of-style:writing-clearly-and-concisely skill if available
- Commit the design document to git

**Spec Review Loop:**
After writing the spec document:

1. Dispatch spec-document-reviewer subagent (see spec-document-reviewer-prompt.md)
2. If Issues Found: fix, re-dispatch, repeat until Approved
3. If loop exceeds 3 iterations, surface to human for guidance

**User Review Gate:**
After the spec review loop passes, ask the user to review the written spec before proceeding:

> "Spec written and committed to `<path>`. Please review it and let me know if you want to make any changes before we start writing out the implementation plan."

Wait for the user's response. If they request changes, make them and re-run the spec review loop. Only proceed once the user approves.

**Implementation:**

- Invoke the writing-plans skill to create a detailed implementation plan
- Do NOT invoke any other skill. writing-plans is the next step.

## Key Principles

- **One question at a time** - Don't overwhelm with multiple questions
- **Multiple choice preferred** - Easier to answer than open-ended when possible
- **YAGNI ruthlessly** - Remove unnecessary features from all designs
- **Explore alternatives** - Always propose 2-3 approaches before settling
- **Incremental validation** - Present design, get approval before moving on
- **Be flexible** - Go back and clarify when something doesn't make sense

## Visual Companion

A browser-based companion for showing mockups, diagrams, and visual options during brainstorming. Available as a tool — not a mode. Accepting the companion means it's available for questions that benefit from visual treatment; it does NOT mean every question goes through the browser.

**Offering the companion:** When you anticipate that upcoming questions will involve visual content (mockups, layouts, diagrams), offer it once for consent:
> "Some of what we're working on might be easier to explain if I can show it to you in a web browser. I can put together mockups, diagrams, comparisons, and other visuals as we go. This feature is still new and can be token-intensive. Want to try it? (Requires opening a local URL)"

**This offer MUST be its own message.** Do not combine it with clarifying questions, context summaries, or any other content. The message should contain ONLY the offer above and nothing else. Wait for the user's response before continuing. If they decline, proceed with text-only brainstorming.

**Per-question decision:** Even after the user accepts, decide FOR EACH QUESTION whether to use the browser or the terminal. The test: **would the user understand this better by seeing it than reading it?**

- **Use the browser** for content that IS visual — mockups, wireframes, layout comparisons, architecture diagrams, side-by-side visual designs
- **Use the terminal** for content that is text — requirements questions, conceptual choices, tradeoff lists, A/B/C/D text options, scope decisions

A question about a UI topic is not automatically a visual question. "What does personality mean in this context?" is a conceptual question — use the terminal. "Which wizard layout works better?" is a visual question — use the browser.

If they agree to the companion, read the detailed guide before proceeding:
`skills/brainstorming/visual-companion.md`

input.prompt:
Explore the OpenACP codebase at /Users/kienduong/works/ai-agents/OpenACP to understand how sessions and topics are managed. Thoroughness: very thorough.

I need to understand:

1. **Session lifecycle**: How sessions are created, stored in memory, and destroyed. Look at `src/core/session.ts`, `src/core/session-manager.ts`, `src/core/core.ts`

2. **Topic lifecycle**: How Telegram forum topics are created and linked to sessions. Look at `src/adapters/telegram/adapter.ts`, `src/adapters/telegram/commands.ts`

3. **Session-to-topic mapping**: How threadId is associated with a session, where it's stored, and whether any persistence exists

4. **What happens on restart**: Is there any existing persistence mechanism (file, DB, etc.)? Any existing state recovery logic?

5. **Session data model**: What fields does a Session have? What's needed to reconstruct a session after restart?

6. **Agent subprocess**: How AgentInstance is spawned and connected — can an agent subprocess survive a restart or must it be re-spawned?

Provide the key findings with file paths and line numbers.

1→import { ConfigManager } from './config.js'
     2→import { AgentManager } from './agent-manager.js'
     3→import { SessionManager } from './session-manager.js'
     4→import { NotificationManager } from './notification.js'
     5→import { ChannelAdapter } from './channel.js'
     6→import { Session } from './session.js'
     7→import type { IncomingMessage, AgentEvent, OutgoingMessage, PermissionRequest } from './types.js'
     8→import { createChildLogger } from './log.js'
     9→const log = createChildLogger({ module: 'core' })
    10→
    11→export class OpenACPCore {
    12→  configManager: ConfigManager
    13→  agentManager: AgentManager
    14→  sessionManager: SessionManager
    15→  notificationManager: NotificationManager
    16→  adapters: Map<string, ChannelAdapter> = new Map()
    17→
    18→  constructor(configManager: ConfigManager) {
    19→    this.configManager = configManager
    20→    const config = configManager.get()
    21→    this.agentManager = new AgentManager(config)
    22→    this.sessionManager = new SessionManager()
    23→    this.notificationManager = new NotificationManager(this.adapters)
    24→  }
    25→
    26→  registerAdapter(name: string, adapter: ChannelAdapter): void {
    27→    this.adapters.set(name, adapter)
    28→  }
    29→
    30→  async start(): Promise<void> {
    31→    for (const adapter of this.adapters.values()) {
    32→      await adapter.start()
    33→    }
    34→  }
    35→
    36→  async stop(): Promise<void> {
    37→    // 1. Notify users
    38→    try {
    39→      await this.notificationManager.notifyAll({
    40→        sessionId: 'system',
    41→        type: 'error',
    42→        summary: 'OpenACP is shutting down',
    43→      })
    44→    } catch { /* best effort */ }
    45→
    46→    // 2. Destroy all sessions
    47→    await this.sessionManager.destroyAll()
    48→
    49→    // 3. Stop adapters
    50→    for (const adapter of this.adapters.values()) {
    51→      await adapter.stop()
    52→    }
    53→  }
    54→
    55→  // --- Message Routing ---
    56→
    57→  async handleMessage(message: IncomingMessage): Promise<void> {
    58→    const config = this.configManager.get()
    59→    log.debug({ channelId: message.channelId, threadId: message.threadId, userId: message.userId }, 'Incoming message')
    60→
    61→    // Security: check allowed user IDs
    62→    if (config.security.allowedUserIds.length > 0) {
    63→      if (!config.security.allowedUserIds.includes(message.userId)) {
    64→        log.warn({ userId: message.userId }, 'Rejected message from unauthorized user')
    65→        return
    66→      }
    67→    }
    68→
    69→    // Check concurrent session limit
    70→    const activeSessions = this.sessionManager.listSessions()
    71→      .filter(s => s.status === 'active' || s.status === 'initializing')
    72→    if (activeSessions.length >= config.security.maxConcurrentSessions) {
    73→      log.warn({ userId: message.userId, currentCount: activeSessions.length, max: config.security.maxConcurrentSessions }, 'Session limit reached')
    74→      const adapter = this.adapters.get(message.channelId)
    75→      if (adapter) {
    76→        await adapter.sendMessage('system', {
    77→          type: 'error',
    78→          text: `Max concurrent sessions (${config.security.maxConcurrentSessions}) reached. Cancel a session first.`,
    79→        })
    80→      }
    81→      return
    82→    }
    83→
    84→    // Find session by thread
    85→    const session = this.sessionManager.getSessionByThread(message.channelId, message.threadId)
    86→    if (!session) return
    87→
    88→    // Forward to session
    89→    await session.enqueuePrompt(message.text)
    90→  }
    91→
    92→  async handleNewSession(
    93→    channelId: string,
    94→    agentName?: string,
    95→    workspacePath?: string,
    96→  ): Promise<Session> {
    97→    const config = this.configManager.get()
    98→    const resolvedAgent = agentName || config.defaultAgent
    99→    log.info({ channelId, agentName: resolvedAgent }, 'New session request')
   100→    const resolvedWorkspace = this.configManager.resolveWorkspace(
   101→      workspacePath || config.agents[resolvedAgent]?.workingDirectory
   102→    )
   103→
   104→    const session = await this.sessionManager.createSession(
   105→      channelId, resolvedAgent, resolvedWorkspace, this.agentManager
   106→    )
   107→
   108→    // Wire events
   109→    const adapter = this.adapters.get(channelId)
   110→    if (adapter) {
   111→      this.wireSessionEvents(session, adapter)
   112→    }
   113→
   114→    return session
   115→  }
   116→
   117→  async handleNewChat(
   118→    channelId: string,
   119→    currentThreadId: string,
   120→  ): Promise<Session | null> {
   121→    const currentSession = this.sessionManager.getSessionByThread(channelId, currentThreadId)
   122→    if (!currentSession) return null
   123→
   124→    return this.handleNewSession(
   125→      channelId,
   126→      currentSession.agentName,
   127→      currentSession.workingDirectory,
   128→    )
   129→  }
   130→
   131→  // --- Event Wiring ---
   132→
   133→  private toOutgoingMessage(event: AgentEvent): OutgoingMessage {
   134→    switch (event.type) {
   135→      case 'text':
   136→        return { type: 'text', text: event.content }
   137→      case 'thought':
   138→        return { type: 'thought', text: event.content }
   139→      case 'tool_call':
   140→        return { type: 'tool_call', text: event.name, metadata: { id: event.id, kind: event.kind, status: event.status, content: event.content, locations: event.locations } }
   141→      case 'tool_update':
   142→        return { type: 'tool_update', text: '', metadata: { id: event.id, status: event.status, content: event.content } }
   143→      case 'plan':
   144→        return { type: 'plan', text: '', metadata: { entries: event.entries } }
   145→      case 'usage':
   146→        return { type: 'usage', text: '', metadata: { tokensUsed: event.tokensUsed, contextSize: event.contextSize, cost: event.cost } }
   147→      default:
   148→        return { type: 'text', text: '' }
   149→    }
   150→  }
   151→
   152→  // Public — adapters call this for assistant session wiring
   153→  wireSessionEvents(session: Session, adapter: ChannelAdapter): void {
   154→    // Set adapter reference for autoName → renameSessionThread
   155→    session.adapter = adapter
   156→
   157→    session.agentInstance.onSessionUpdate = (event: AgentEvent) => {
   158→      switch (event.type) {
   159→        case 'text':
   160→        case 'thought':
   161→        case 'tool_call':
   162→        case 'tool_update':
   163→        case 'plan':
   164→        case 'usage':
   165→          adapter.sendMessage(session.id, this.toOutgoingMessage(event))
   166→          break
   167→
   168→        case 'session_end':
   169→          session.status = 'finished'
   170→          adapter.cleanupSkillCommands(session.id)
   171→          adapter.sendMessage(session.id, { type: 'session_end', text: `Done (${event.reason})` })
   172→          this.notificationManager.notify(session.channelId, {
   173→            sessionId: session.id,
   174→            sessionName: session.name,
   175→            type: 'completed',
   176→            summary: `Session "${session.name || session.id}" completed`,
   177→          })
   178→          break
   179→
   180→        case 'error':
   181→          adapter.cleanupSkillCommands(session.id)
   182→          adapter.sendMessage(session.id, { type: 'error', text: event.message })
   183→          this.notificationManager.notify(session.channelId, {
   184→            sessionId: session.id,
   185→            sessionName: session.name,
   186→            type: 'error',
   187→            summary: event.message,
   188→          })
   189→          break
   190→
   191→        case 'commands_update':
   192→          log.debug({ commands: event.commands }, 'Commands available')
   193→          adapter.sendSkillCommands(session.id, event.commands)
   194→          break
   195→      }
   196→    }
   197→
   198→    session.agentInstance.onPermissionRequest = async (request: PermissionRequest) => {
   199→      // Set pending BEFORE sending UI to avoid race condition
   200→      const promise = new Promise<string>((resolve) => {
   201→        session.pendingPermission = { requestId: request.id, resolve }
   202→      })
   203→
   204→      // Send permission UI to session topic (notification is sent by adapter)
   205→      await adapter.sendPermissionRequest(session.id, request)
   206→
   207→      // Wait for user response — adapter resolves this promise
   208→      return promise
   209→    }
   210→  }
   211→}
   212→

1→import type { Bot } from 'grammy'
     2→
     3→// Ensure notification and assistant topics exist, create if needed
     4→// Returns updated topic IDs
     5→export async function ensureTopics(
     6→  bot: Bot,
     7→  chatId: number,
     8→  config: { notificationTopicId: number | null; assistantTopicId: number | null },
     9→  saveConfig: (updates: { notificationTopicId?: number; assistantTopicId?: number }) => Promise<void>,
    10→): Promise<{ notificationTopicId: number; assistantTopicId: number }> {
    11→  let notificationTopicId = config.notificationTopicId
    12→  let assistantTopicId = config.assistantTopicId
    13→
    14→  if (notificationTopicId === null) {
    15→    const topic = await bot.api.createForumTopic(chatId, '📋 Notifications')
    16→    notificationTopicId = topic.message_thread_id
    17→    await saveConfig({ notificationTopicId })
    18→  }
    19→
    20→  if (assistantTopicId === null) {
    21→    const topic = await bot.api.createForumTopic(chatId, '🤖 Assistant')
    22→    assistantTopicId = topic.message_thread_id
    23→    await saveConfig({ assistantTopicId })
    24→  }
    25→
    26→  return { notificationTopicId, assistantTopicId }
    27→}
    28→
    29→// Create a new forum topic for a session
    30→export async function createSessionTopic(
    31→  bot: Bot,
    32→  chatId: number,
    33→  name: string,
    34→): Promise<number> {
    35→  const topic = await bot.api.createForumTopic(chatId, name)
    36→  return topic.message_thread_id

    39→// Rename an existing forum topic
    40→export async function renameSessionTopic(
    41→  bot: Bot,
    42→  chatId: number,
    43→  threadId: number,
    44→  name: string,
    45→): Promise<void> {
    46→  try {
    47→    await bot.api.editForumTopic(chatId, threadId, { name })
    48→  } catch {
    49→    // Ignore rename failures (topic may be closed/deleted)
    50→  }
    51→}
    52→
    53→// Build a Telegram deep link to a specific message
    54→export function buildDeepLink(chatId: number, messageId: number): string {
    55→  // chatId for supergroups starts with -100, need to strip it for the link
    56→  const cleanId = String(chatId).replace('-100', '')
    57→  return `https://t.me/c/${cleanId}/${messageId}`

1→import { z } from 'zod'
     2→import * as fs from 'node:fs'
     3→import * as path from 'node:path'
     4→import * as os from 'node:os'
     5→import { createChildLogger } from './log.js'
     6→const log = createChildLogger({ module: 'config' })
     7→
     8→const BaseChannelSchema = z.object({
     9→  enabled: z.boolean().default(false),
    10→  adapter: z.string().optional(),  // package name for plugin adapters
    11→}).passthrough()
    12→
    13→export const PLUGINS_DIR = path.join(os.homedir(), '.openacp', 'plugins')
    14→
    15→const AgentSchema = z.object({
    16→  command: z.string(),
    17→  args: z.array(z.string()).default([]),
    18→  workingDirectory: z.string().optional(),
    19→  env: z.record(z.string(), z.string()).default({}),
    20→})
    21→
    22→const LoggingSchema = z.object({
    23→  level: z.enum(['silent', 'debug', 'info', 'warn', 'error', 'fatal']).default('info'),
    24→  logDir: z.string().default('~/.openacp/logs'),
    25→  maxFileSize: z.union([z.string(), z.number()]).default('10m'),
    26→  maxFiles: z.number().default(7),
    27→  sessionLogRetentionDays: z.number().default(30),
    28→}).default({})
    29→
    30→export type LoggingConfig = z.infer<typeof LoggingSchema>
    31→
    32→export const ConfigSchema = z.object({
    33→  channels: z.record(z.string(), BaseChannelSchema),
    34→  agents: z.record(z.string(), AgentSchema),
    35→  defaultAgent: z.string(),
    36→  workspace: z.object({
    37→    baseDir: z.string().default('~/openacp-workspace'),
    38→  }).default({}),
    39→  security: z.object({
    40→    allowedUserIds: z.array(z.string()).default([]),
    41→    maxConcurrentSessions: z.number().default(5),
    42→    sessionTimeoutMinutes: z.number().default(60),
    43→  }).default({}),
    44→  logging: LoggingSchema,
    45→})
    46→
    47→export type Config = z.infer<typeof ConfigSchema>
    48→
    49→export function expandHome(p: string): string {
    50→  if (p.startsWith('~')) {
    51→    return path.join(os.homedir(), p.slice(1))
    52→  }
    53→  return p
    54→}
    55→
    56→const DEFAULT_CONFIG = {
    57→  channels: {
    58→    telegram: {
    59→      enabled: false,
    60→      botToken: "YOUR_BOT_TOKEN_HERE",
    61→      chatId: 0,
    62→      notificationTopicId: null,
    63→      assistantTopicId: null
    64→    }
    65→  },
    66→  agents: {
    67→    claude: { command: "claude-agent-acp", args: [], env: {} },
    68→    codex: { command: "codex", args: ["--acp"], env: {} }
    69→  },
    70→  defaultAgent: "claude",
    71→  workspace: { baseDir: "~/openacp-workspace" },
    72→  security: { allowedUserIds: [], maxConcurrentSessions: 5, sessionTimeoutMinutes: 60 }
    73→}
    74→
    75→export class ConfigManager {
    76→  private config!: Config
    77→  private configPath: string
    78→
    79→  constructor() {
    80→    this.configPath = process.env.OPENACP_CONFIG_PATH || expandHome('~/.openacp/config.json')
    81→  }
    82→
    83→  async load(): Promise<void> {
    84→    // 1. Ensure directory exists
    85→    const dir = path.dirname(this.configPath)
    86→    fs.mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true })
    87→
    88→    // 2. If config file doesn't exist, create default
    89→    if (!fs.existsSync(this.configPath)) {
    90→      fs.writeFileSync(this.configPath, JSON.stringify(DEFAULT_CONFIG, null, 2))
    91→      log.info({ configPath: this.configPath }, 'Config created')
    92→      log.info('Please edit it with your Telegram bot token and chat ID, then restart.')
    93→      process.exit(1)
    94→    }
    95→
    96→    // 3. Read and parse
    97→    const raw = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(this.configPath, 'utf-8'))
    98→
    99→    // 4. Apply env var overrides
   100→    this.applyEnvOverrides(raw)
   101→
   102→    // 5. Validate with Zod
   103→    const result = ConfigSchema.safeParse(raw)
   104→    if (!result.success) {
   105→      log.error('Config validation failed')
   106→      for (const issue of result.error.issues) {
   107→        log.error({ path: issue.path.join('.'), message: issue.message }, 'Validation error')
   108→      }
   109→      process.exit(1)
   110→    }
   111→    this.config = result.data
   112→  }
   113→
   114→  get(): Config {
   115→    return this.config
   116→  }
   117→
   118→  async save(updates: Record<string, unknown>): Promise<void> {
   119→    // Read current file, merge updates, write back
   120→    const raw = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(this.configPath, 'utf-8'))
   121→    this.deepMerge(raw, updates)
   122→    fs.writeFileSync(this.configPath, JSON.stringify(raw, null, 2))
   123→    // Re-validate and update in-memory config
   124→    const result = ConfigSchema.safeParse(raw)
   125→    if (result.success) {
   126→      this.config = result.data
   127→    }
   128→  }
   129→
   130→  resolveWorkspace(input?: string): string {
   131→    if (!input) {
   132→      const resolved = expandHome(this.config.workspace.baseDir)
   133→      fs.mkdirSync(resolved, { recursive: true })
   134→      return resolved
   135→    }
   136→    if (input.startsWith('/') || input.startsWith('~')) {
   137→      const resolved = expandHome(input)
   138→      fs.mkdirSync(resolved, { recursive: true })
   139→      return resolved
   140→    }
   141→    // Named workspace → lowercase, under baseDir
   142→    const name = input.toLowerCase()
   143→    const resolved = path.join(expandHome(this.config.workspace.baseDir), name)
   144→    fs.mkdirSync(resolved, { recursive: true })
   145→    return resolved
   146→  }
   147→
   148→  async exists(): Promise<boolean> {
   149→    return fs.existsSync(this.configPath)
   150→  }

     2→
     3→import { ConfigManager } from './core/config.js'
     4→import { OpenACPCore } from './core/core.js'
     5→import { loadAdapterFactory } from './core/plugin-manager.js'
     6→import { initLogger, shutdownLogger, cleanupOldSessionLogs, log } from './core/log.js'
     7→import { TelegramAdapter } from './adapters/telegram/index.js'
     8→
     9→let shuttingDown = false
    10→
    11→export async function startServer() {
    12→  // 1. Check config exists, run setup if not
    13→  const configManager = new ConfigManager()
    14→  const configExists = await configManager.exists()
    15→
    16→  if (!configExists) {
    17→    const { runSetup } = await import('./core/setup.js')
    18→    const shouldStart = await runSetup(configManager)
    19→    if (!shouldStart) process.exit(0)
    20→  }
    21→
    22→  // 2. Load config (validates with Zod)
    23→  await configManager.load()
    24→  const config = configManager.get()
    25→  initLogger(config.logging)
    26→  log.info({ configPath: configManager.getConfigPath() }, 'Config loaded')
    27→
    28→  // Async cleanup of old session logs (non-blocking)
    29→  cleanupOldSessionLogs(config.logging.sessionLogRetentionDays).catch(err =>
    30→    log.warn({ err }, 'Session log cleanup failed')
    31→  )
    32→
    33→  // 3. Create core
    34→  const core = new OpenACPCore(configManager)
    35→
    36→  // 4. Register adapters from config
    37→  for (const [channelName, channelConfig] of Object.entries(config.channels)) {
    38→    if (!channelConfig.enabled) continue
    39→
    40→    if (channelName === 'telegram') {
    41→      core.registerAdapter('telegram', new TelegramAdapter(core, channelConfig as any))
    42→      log.info({ adapter: 'telegram' }, 'Adapter registered')
    43→    } else if (channelConfig.adapter) {
    44→      // Plugin adapter
    45→      const factory = await loadAdapterFactory(channelConfig.adapter)
    46→      if (factory) {
    47→        const adapter = factory.createAdapter(core, channelConfig)
    48→        core.registerAdapter(channelName, adapter)
    49→        log.info({ adapter: channelName, plugin: channelConfig.adapter }, 'Adapter registered')
    50→      } else {
    51→        const name = channelName
    52→        const err = channelConfig.adapter
    53→        log.error({ adapter: name, err }, 'Failed to load adapter')
    54→      }
    55→    } else {
    56→      log.error({ adapter: channelName }, 'Channel has no built-in adapter; set "adapter" field to a plugin package')
    57→    }
    58→  }
    59→
    60→  if (core.adapters.size === 0) {
    61→    log.error('No channels enabled. Enable at least one channel in config.')
    62→    process.exit(1)
    63→  }
    64→
    65→  // 5. Start
    66→  await core.start()
    67→
    68→  // 6. Log ready
    69→  const agents = Object.keys(config.agents)
    70→  log.info({ agents }, 'OpenACP started')
    71→  log.info('Press Ctrl+C to stop')
    72→
    73→  // 7. Graceful shutdown
    74→  const shutdown = async (signal: string) => {
    75→    if (shuttingDown) return
    76→    shuttingDown = true
    77→    log.info({ signal }, 'Signal received, shutting down')
    78→
    79→    try {
    80→      await core.stop()
    81→    } catch (err) {
    82→      log.error({ err }, 'Error during shutdown')
    83→    }
    84→
    85→    await shutdownLogger()
    86→    process.exit(0)
    87→  }
    88→
    89→  process.on('SIGINT', () => shutdown('SIGINT'))
    90→  process.on('SIGTERM', () => shutdown('SIGTERM'))
    91→
    92→  process.on('uncaughtException', (err) => {
    93→    log.error({ err }, 'Uncaught exception')
    94→  })
    95→
    96→  process.on('unhandledRejection', (err) => {
    97→    log.error({ err }, 'Unhandled rejection')
    98→  })
    99→}
   100→
   101→// Direct execution for dev (node dist/main.js)
   102→const isDirectExecution = process.argv[1]?.endsWith('main.js')
   103→if (isDirectExecution) {
   104→  startServer().catch((err) => {
   105→    log.error({ err }, 'Fatal error')
   106→    process.exit(1)
   107→  })
   108→}
   109→

## Key Findings: Session & Topic Lifecycle in OpenACP

### 1. **Session Lifecycle: Creation, Storage, and Destruction**

**File: `/Users/kienduong/works/ai-agents/OpenACP/src/core/session.ts`**

- **Session class** (lines 8-138):
  - Each session has: `id` (nanoid-generated, 12 chars), `channelId`, `threadId`, `agentName`, `workingDirectory`, `agentInstance`, `status`, `name`, `createdAt`
  - `threadId` starts empty (line 11: `threadId: string = ''`) and is set later by adapter
  - Session maintains a `promptQueue` for serial prompt processing
  - Sessions can be in states: `initializing`, `active`, `cancelled`, `finished`, `error`
  - All sessions destroyed via `Session.destroy()` which calls `agentInstance.destroy()` (lines 134-137)

**File: `/Users/kienduong/works/ai-agents/OpenACP/src/core/session-manager.ts`**

- **SessionManager class** (lines 4-49):
  - Sessions stored in **in-memory Map**: `private sessions: Map<string, Session> = new Map()` (line 5)
  - **No persistence layer** — sessions are ephemeral
  - `createSession()` spawns agent subprocess and stores session in Map (lines 7-16)
  - `getSessionByThread()` looks up session by `channelId + threadId` (lines 23-30)
  - `destroyAll()` clears all sessions on shutdown (lines 43-48)

---

### 2. **Topic Lifecycle: Creation and Linking to Sessions**

**File: `/Users/kienduong/works/ai-agents/OpenACP/src/adapters/telegram/topics.ts`**

- **Forum topics** are Telegram's mechanism for organizing conversation threads
- `ensureTopics()` (lines 5-27): Creates system topics on first start if not in config
  - Notification topic (`📋 Notifications`)
  - Assistant topic (`🤖 Assistant`)
  - Topic IDs **persisted in config** (`~/.openacp/config.json`)
- `createSessionTopic()` (lines 30-37): Creates a new forum topic, returns `message_thread_id`
- `renameSessionTopic()` (lines 40-51): Renames an existing topic

**File: `/Users/kienduong/works/ai-agents/OpenACP/src/adapters/telegram/commands.ts`**

- **Session creation flow** (`handleNew()`, lines 80-144):
  1. Create forum topic first with temporary name `"🔄 New Session"` (line 97)
  2. Get `threadId` from topic creation (line 97)
  3. **Send "setting up" message to topic** before session spawning (lines 100-103)
  4. Call `core.handleNewSession()` to create session (lines 105-109)
  5. **Set `session.threadId = String(threadId)`** (line 110) — **THIS IS CRITICAL**
  6. Rename topic with agent name (lines 115-118)

- **Topic-first pattern is essential**: Topics must exist before session events fire to prevent race conditions (comment lines 93-94)

---

### 3. **Session-to-Topic Mapping: ThreadId Association**

**Flow in `/Users/kienduong/works/ai-agents/OpenACP/src/core/core.ts`**

- `handleNewSession()` (lines 92-115):
  - Creates session via `sessionManager.createSession()`
  - Returns session with empty `threadId`
  - Caller (Telegram adapter) sets `session.threadId = String(threadId)` after topic creation
  
- `wireSessionEvents()` (lines 153-210):
  - Sets up event listeners on `agentInstance`
  - When agent emits events, adapter calls `sendMessage(session.id, message)`
  - Adapter retrieves session by ID to get `session.threadId` and post to Telegram topic

**File: `/Users/kienduong/works/ai-agents/OpenACP/src/adapters/telegram/adapter.ts`**

- `sendMessage()` (lines 241-401):
  - Takes `sessionId`, looks up session: `core.sessionManager.getSession(sessionId)` (line 246)
  - Extracts `threadId = Number(session.threadId)` (line 250)
  - Posts messages to that thread ID (e.g., line 286: `message_thread_id: threadId`)

- Lookup by thread is via `SessionManager.getSessionByThread(channelId, threadId)` (session-manager.ts, lines 23-30)

---

### 4. **What Happens on Restart: No Persistence**

**Current State**: **NO persistence mechanism exists**

- All sessions stored in memory only
- On process restart → `SessionManager.sessions` is cleared
- Telegram forum topics remain (they're Telegram data)
- But OpenACP has no way to recover session state:
  - Session IDs are lost (can't map old topics to sessions)
  - Agent subprocesses are killed and must be respawned
  - Conversation history is in the agent subprocess (no disk backup)

**Existing persistence**: Only **topic IDs for system topics** are saved to config
- File: `/Users/kienduong/works/ai-agents/OpenACP/src/adapters/telegram/adapter.ts` (lines 100-110)
- `notificationTopicId` and `assistantTopicId` saved to `~/.openacp/config.json`
- No session metadata is persisted

---

### 5. **Session Data Model: What's Stored in Memory**

**Session fields** (session.ts, lines 8-22):
```typescript
id: string                                    // nanoid(12)
channelId: string                            // "telegram"
threadId: string                             // Set by adapter after topic creation
agentName: string                            // e.g., "claude"
workingDirectory: string                     // e.g., ~/openacp-workspace/project
agentInstance: AgentInstance                 // Subprocess connection (NOT serializable)
status: SessionStatus                        // 'initializing' | 'active' | 'cancelled' | 'finished' | 'error'
name?: string                                // Auto-generated after first prompt
promptQueue: string[]                        // Queued user messages
promptRunning: boolean                       // Is agent currently processing?
createdAt: Date                              // Session creation timestamp
adapter?: ChannelAdapter                     // Reference back to adapter
pendingPermission?: { requestId, resolve }   // For permission request callbacks
log: Logger                                  // Session-scoped logger
```

**Non-serializable fields**:
- `agentInstance` — contains spawned child process, ACP connection, stream handlers
- `adapter` — contains bot instance, callbacks
- `pendingPermission.resolve` — Promise resolver function

---

### 6. **Agent Subprocess: Cannot Survive Restart**

**File: `/Users/kienduong/works/ai-agents/OpenACP/src/core/agent-instance.ts`**

- **AgentInstance spawns a subprocess** via `spawn()` (lines 84-174)
  - Creates child process with stdio pipes (line 93)
  - Establishes ACP protocol connection over stdout/stdin (lines 130-137)
  - Creates an ACP session (lines 150-153)
  - Process tied to parent lifecycle (line 157-166): Listens for `exit` event
  
- **Cannot be serialized or recovered**:
  - Process state is in kernel memory
  - Stdio pipes are not transferable across process boundaries
  - ACP session ID is ephemeral (created per connection)
  - No checkpoint/restore mechanism

---

### 7. **Existing Persistence: Only Config-Level Topic IDs**

**File: `/Users/kienduong/works/ai-agents/OpenACP/src/adapters/telegram/adapter.ts` (lines 100-110)**

```typescript
const topics = await ensureTopics(
  this.bot,
  this.telegramConfig.chatId,
  this.telegramConfig,
  async (updates) => {
    // Save topic IDs to config
    await (this.core as OpenACPCore).configManager.save({
      channels: { telegram: updates },
    });
  },
);
```

**ConfigManager.save()** (config.ts, lines 118-128):
- Reads `~/.openacp/config.json`
- Merges updates (e.g., `notificationTopicId: 123`)
- Writes back to disk

**This is the only persistence** — system topic IDs only, no session data

---

## Summary Table

| Aspect | Location | Status |
|--------|----------|--------|
| Session storage | `SessionManager.sessions` (Map) | In-memory only |
| ThreadId association | `Session.threadId` set by adapter | In-memory reference |
| ThreadId lookup | `SessionManager.getSessionByThread()` | In-memory scan |
| Topic creation | `TelegramAdapter.handleNew()` + `createSessionTopic()` | Creates Telegram topics dynamically |
| System topic persistence | `ConfigManager.save()` → `~/.openacp/config.json` | Persisted (notification, assistant topics only) |
| Session data persistence | None | **MISSING** |
| Agent subprocess | `AgentInstance.spawn()` with child_process | Ephemeral, dies on parent shutdown |
| Crash detection | `AgentInstance.child.on('exit')` | Triggers error event, session marked 'error' |

---

## Key Bottlenecks for Session Recovery on Restart

1. **No session metadata file** — can't know which Telegram topics correspond to which sessions
2. **No way to reconnect to old subprocesses** — they're killed and would need full re-spawn anyway
3. **No conversation history backup** — stored only in agent subprocess memory
4. **No session-to-thread index on disk** — must create topics dynamically

All of this information is needed to design a persistence and recovery strategy.

content.text:
Tổng hợp tình hình hiện tại:

- **Session** lưu hoàn toàn trong memory (`Map<string, Session>`)
- **Telegram topics** vẫn tồn tại sau restart (data phía Telegram), nhưng OpenACP mất mapping session ↔ topic
- **Agent subprocess** (claude-agent-acp) bị kill khi restart → không thể reconnect, phải spawn lại
- **Conversation history** nằm trong agent subprocess → mất khi restart
- Hiện chỉ persist **system topic IDs** (notification, assistant) vào config.json

Câu hỏi đầu tiên:

**Khi restart, bạn mong muốn điều gì xảy ra với các session cũ?**

A. **Resume session** — Reconnect vào topic cũ, spawn lại agent, user có thể tiếp tục chat (nhưng agent mất conversation history)
B. **Archive & cleanup** — Đánh dấu topic cũ là "ended", user phải tạo session mới
C. **Resume với history** — Như A nhưng cũng lưu conversation history để feed lại cho agent khi re-spawn (phức tạp hơn nhiều)

1→# ACP adapter for the Claude Agent SDK
     2→
     3→[![npm](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/%40zed-industries%2Fclaude-agent-acp)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@zed-industries/claude-agent-acp)
     4→
     5→Use [Claude Agent SDK](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/agent-sdk/overview#branding-guidelines) from [ACP-compatible](https://agentclientprotocol.com) clients such as [Zed](https://zed.dev)!
     6→
     7→This tool implements an ACP agent by using the official [Claude Agent SDK](https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/agent-sdk/overview), supporting:
     8→
     9→- Context @-mentions
    10→- Images
    11→- Tool calls (with permission requests)
    12→- Following
    13→- Edit review
    14→- TODO lists
    15→- Interactive (and background) terminals
    16→- Custom [Slash commands](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code/slash-commands)
    17→- Client MCP servers
    18→
    19→Learn more about the [Agent Client Protocol](https://agentclientprotocol.com/).
    20→
    21→## How to use
    22→
    23→### Zed
    24→
    25→The latest version of Zed can already use this adapter out of the box.
    26→
    27→To use Claude Agent, open the Agent Panel and click "New Claude Agent Thread" from the `+` button menu in the top-right:
    28→
    29→https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ddce66c7-79ac-47a3-ad59-4a6a3ca74903
    30→
    31→Read the docs on [External Agent](https://zed.dev/docs/ai/external-agents) support.
    32→
    33→### Other clients
    34→
    35→Or try it with any of the other [ACP compatible clients](https://agentclientprotocol.com/overview/clients)!
    36→
    37→#### Installation
    38→
    39→Install the adapter from `npm`:
    40→
    41→```bash
    42→npm install -g @zed-industries/claude-agent-acp
    43→```
    44→
    45→You can then use `claude-agent-acp` as a regular ACP agent:
    46→
    47→```
    48→ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-... claude-agent-acp
    49→```
    50→
    51→#### Single-file executable
    52→
    53→Pre-built single-file binaries are available on the [Releases](https://github.com/zed-industries/claude-agent-acp/releases) page for Linux, macOS, and Windows.
    54→These binaries bundle everything needed and don't require Node.js.
    55→
    56→## License
    57→
    58→Apache-2.0
    59→

## Summary: Conversation History and Session Persistence in @zed-industries/claude-agent-acp

Based on thorough exploration of the ACP package and its dependencies, here are the key findings:

### 1. CONVERSATION HISTORY STORAGE

**Location**: Sessions are stored as JSONL (JSON Lines) transcript files in the Claude Agent SDK's managed directories.

**Storage Path**: The SDK stores sessions in project-specific directories. The `listSessions()` function searches for sessions:
- In the provided project directory (if specified via `dir` parameter)
- In all project directories (if `dir` is omitted)
- File path is accessed via `transcript_path` field in `SDKSessionInfo`

**Storage Format**: JSONL transcript files with chronological message records. Each entry contains:
- Message UUID
- Parent UUID (links for message chain)
- Session ID
- Message type (user/assistant)
- Content (with role, tool calls, etc.)

**File:**
- `/Users/kienduong/works/ai-agents/OpenACP/node_modules/@zed-industries/claude-agent-acp/dist/acp-agent.js` lines 657-671 (replaySessionHistory function)
- `/Users/kienduong/works/ai-agents/OpenACP/node_modules/.pnpm/@anthropic-ai+claude-agent-sdk@0.2.76_zod@3.25.76/node_modules/@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk/sdk.d.ts` lines 442-452 (getSessionMessages function)

---

### 2. SESSION MANAGEMENT

**Session Structure**: In-memory representation of a session in ClaudeAcpAgent:

```typescript
type Session = {
    query: Query;                              // Active Claude Agent SDK query
    input: Pushable<SDKUserMessage>;          // Message queue
    cancelled: boolean;
    cwd: string;                               // Working directory
    settingsManager: SettingsManager;
    accumulatedUsage: AccumulatedUsage;       // Token tracking
    modes: SessionModeState;                   // Permission mode state
    models: SessionModelState;                 // Current model
    configOptions: SessionConfigOption[];
    promptRunning: boolean;
    pendingMessages: Map<string, {...}>;      // Queued prompts
    nextPendingOrder: number;
    abortController: AbortController;
};
```

**File:** `/Users/kienduong/works/ai-agents/OpenACP/node_modules/@zed-industries/claude-agent-acp/dist/acp-agent.d.ts` lines 21-38

**Session Lifecycle**:
1. **New Session** (line 146): `newSession()` creates new sessionId via UUID, calls `createSession()`
2. **Resume** (line 177): `unstable_resumeSession()` loads existing session from disk
3. **Fork** (line 162): `unstable_forkSession()` branches from existing session, creates new ID
4. **Load** (line 185): `loadSession()` + history replay - fully restores conversation
5. **Close** (line 550): `unstable_closeSession()` terminates session and cleans up

---

### 3. ACP PROTOCOL HANDLING

**Capabilities Advertised** (line 111-131):
```javascript
agentCapabilities: {
    promptCapabilities: { image: true, embeddedContext: true },
    mcpCapabilities: { http: true, sse: true },
    loadSession: true,
    sessionCapabilities: { fork: {}, list: {}, resume: {}, close: {} }
}
```

**Message Flow**:
1. **Input**: ACP client sends `PromptRequest`
2. **Processing**: Converted to `SDKUserMessage` via `promptToClaude()` (line 1266)
3. **Queue**: Messages pushed to `session.input` (streaming input)
4. **Streaming**: SDK Query processes messages and emits stream events
5. **Output**: Converted back to ACP notifications via `toAcpNotifications()` (line 1449)

**Files:**
- `/Users/kienduong/works/ai-agents/OpenACP/node_modules/@zed-industries/claude-agent-acp/dist/acp-agent.js` lines 218-537 (prompt handling)
- `/Users/kienduong/works/ai-agents/OpenACP/node_modules/@zed-industries/claude-agent-acp/dist/acp-agent.d.ts` lines 154-164 (conversion functions)

---

### 4. RESUME CAPABILITY

**Resume Mechanism**: The package provides full session resumption via `unstable_resumeSession()`:

**Process** (lines 842-858):
1. Check if session already exists in memory
2. If not, call `createSession()` with `creationOpts.resume = sessionId`
3. SDK loads the session from disk via `getSessionMessages()`
4. Conversation history is NOT automatically replayed (only on explicit `loadSession()`)

**Resume vs. LoadSession**:
- **`unstable_resumeSession()`** (line 177): Reconnects to session, ready for new prompts
- **`loadSession()`** (line 185): Calls resume + `replaySessionHistory()` to show full history

**Fork Support** (line 162): `unstable_forkSession()` creates branching sessions:
- Passes `creationOpts = { resume: sourceSessionId, forkSession: true }`
- Generates new UUID for forked session
- Copies transcript messages into new session file

**Files:**
- `/Users/kienduong/works/ai-agents/OpenACP/node_modules/@zed-industries/claude-agent-acp/dist/acp-agent.js` lines 842-878 (createSession with resume)
- SDK: `forkSession()` function in sdk.d.ts lines 388-400 copies transcript with remapped UUIDs

---

### 5. CLAUDE API INTERACTION

**API Layer**: The package wraps the Claude Agent SDK's `query()` function:

**Query Creation** (lines 1007-1010):
```javascript
const q = query({
    prompt: input,  // Pushable stream of user messages
    options: {...}  // Configuration object
});
```

**Message History Management**:
- **On Resume**: SDK internally loads transcript via JSONL file
- **In Memory**: Active session maintains `Query` object that streams messages
- **Accumulation**: Token usage tracked across response (lines 320-325):
  ```javascript
  session.accumulatedUsage.inputTokens += message.usage.input_tokens;
  session.accumulatedUsage.outputTokens += message.usage.output_tokens;
  // etc.
  ```

**Streaming Architecture** (line 254-255):
- Prompt queue: `session.input` (Pushable stream)
- Response stream: `session.query.next()` async generator
- Multi-turn support: Queued prompts handled via `pendingMessages` map

**Files:**
- `/Users/kienduong/works/ai-agents/OpenACP/node_modules/@zed-industries/claude-agent-acp/dist/acp-agent.js` lines 218-537 (prompt loop)
- Lines 866-1090 (createSession & query configuration)

---

### 6. CONFIGURATION

**Settings Locations** (SettingsManager - lines 880-883):
1. User settings: `~/.claude/settings.json`
2. Project settings: `<cwd>/.claude/settings.json`
3. Local settings: `<cwd>/.claude/settings.local.json`
4. Enterprise managed settings (platform-specific)

**History-Related Options**:
- `resume?: string` - Session ID to resume (line 1083 in SDK)
- `forkSession?: boolean` - Fork on resume (line 884 in SDK)
- `sessionId?: string` - Custom session ID (line 1085-1087 in SDK)
- `maxTurns?: number` - Limit conversation turns (line 975-978 in SDK)

**Configuration Source**:
- File: `/Users/kienduong/works/ai-agents/OpenACP/node_modules/@zed-industries/claude-agent-acp/dist/settings.d.ts` lines 12-19
- Implementation: `/Users/kienduong/works/ai-agents/OpenACP/node_modules/@zed-industries/claude-agent-acp/dist/settings.js`

**Environment Variables**:
- `CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR` - Override config directory (line 11)
- `MAX_THINKING_TOKENS` - Thinking budget (line 925)
- `CLAUDE_CODE_EXECUTABLE` - Path to Claude Code binary (line 959-963)

---

### 7. KEY INSIGHTS

1. **Disk-Based Persistence**: Sessions are persisted as JSONL files managed by the Claude Agent SDK. The ACP package does NOT implement its own session storage.

2. **In-Memory State**: Only active/open sessions are kept in `this.sessions[sessionId]` dictionary. Closed sessions are deleted from memory but remain on disk.

3. **Transparent Resume**: When resuming, the SDK automatically detects and loads the JSONL transcript file by session ID. The ACP layer doesn't manually manage this.

4. **History Replay**: Explicit history replay only happens with `loadSession()` → `replaySessionHistory()`. This converts JSONL records to ACP notifications (lines 657-671).

5. **Multi-Turn Queueing**: The package implements a prompt queue (lines 235-247) to handle concurrent/queued prompts by mapping pending messages and resolving them in order.

6. **No Custom Persistence Layer Needed**: The Claude Agent SDK's `query()` function with `resume` option automatically handles session recovery from disk.

7. **Session Metadata**: Accessible via `listSessions()` which reads session summaries:
   - `sessionId`: UUID
   - `summary`: Auto-generated or custom title
   - `lastModified`: Timestamp (ms)
   - `cwd`: Working directory
   - `createdAt`: Creation timestamp
   - `customTitle`, `firstPrompt`, `gitBranch`, `tag`: Optional metadata

---

### Files Summary

| File | Purpose | Key Lines |
|------|---------|-----------|
| `acp-agent.d.ts` | Type definitions & interfaces | 21-38 (Session type), 154-164 (conversions) |
| `acp-agent.js` | Main ACP implementation | 146-193 (session lifecycle), 218-537 (prompt handling), 657-671 (history replay), 842-878 (createSession), 1007-1010 (query init) |
| `settings.d.ts` | Settings & configuration | 12-19 (ClaudeCodeSettings), 42-105 (SettingsManager) |
| `sdk.d.ts` | Claude Agent SDK types | 452 (getSessionMessages), 531 (listSessions), 1559 (query), 2130-2171 (SDKSessionInfo) |
| `index.js` | Entry point | 5-26 (CLI mode handling) |

**File:** `/Users/kienduong/works/ai-agents/OpenACP/node_modules/@zed-industries/claude-agent-acp/dist/acp-agent.d.ts` lines 21-38

**Session Lifecycle**:
1. **New Session** (line 146): `newSession()` creates new sessionId via UUID, calls `createSession()`
2. **Resume** (line 177): `unstable_resumeSession()` loads existing session from disk
3. **Fork** (line 162): `unstable_forkSession()` branches from existing session, creates new ID
4. **Load** (line 185): `loadSession()` + history replay - fully restores conversation
5. **Close** (line 550): `unstable_closeSession()` terminates session and cleans up

---

### 3. ACP PROTOCOL HANDLING

**Capabilities Advertised** (line 111-131):
```javascript
agentCapabilities: {
    promptCapabilities: { image: true, embeddedContext: true },
    mcpCapabilities: { http: true, sse: true },
    loadSession: true,
    sessionCapabilities: { fork: {}, list: {}, resume: {}, close: {} }
}
```

**Message Flow**:
1. **Input**: ACP client sends `PromptRequest`
2. **Processing**: Converted to `SDKUserMessage` via `promptToClaude()` (line 1266)
3. **Queue**: Messages pushed to `session.input` (streaming input)
4. **Streaming**: SDK Query processes messages and emits stream events
5. **Output**: Converted back to ACP notifications via `toAcpNotifications()` (line 1449)

**Files:**
- `/Users/kienduong/works/ai-agents/OpenACP/node_modules/@zed-industries/claude-agent-acp/dist/acp-agent.js` lines 218-537 (prompt handling)
- `/Users/kienduong/works/ai-agents/OpenACP/node_modules/@zed-industries/claude-agent-acp/dist/acp-agent.d.ts` lines 154-164 (conversion functions)

---

### 4. RESUME CAPABILITY

**Resume Mechanism**: The package provides full session resumption via `unstable_resumeSession()`:

**Process** (lines 842-858):
1. Check if session already exists in memory
2. If not, call `createSession()` with `creationOpts.resume = sessionId`
3. SDK loads the session from disk via `getSessionMessages()`
4. Conversation history is NOT automatically replayed (only on explicit `loadSession()`)

**Resume vs. LoadSession**:
- **`unstable_resumeSession()`** (line 177): Reconnects to session, ready for new prompts
- **`loadSession()`** (line 185): Calls resume + `replaySessionHistory()` to show full history

**Fork Support** (line 162): `unstable_forkSession()` creates branching sessions:
- Passes `creationOpts = { resume: sourceSessionId, forkSession: true }`
- Generates new UUID for forked session
- Copies transcript messages into new session file

**Files:**
- `/Users/kienduong/works/ai-agents/OpenACP/node_modules/@zed-industries/claude-agent-acp/dist/acp-agent.js` lines 842-878 (createSession with resume)
- SDK: `forkSession()` function in sdk.d.ts lines 388-400 copies transcript with remapped UUIDs

---

### 5. CLAUDE API INTERACTION

**API Layer**: The package wraps the Claude Agent SDK's `query()` function:

**Query Creation** (lines 1007-1010):
```javascript
const q = query({
    prompt: input,  // Pushable stream of user messages
    options: {...}  // Configuration object
});
```

**Message History Management**:
- **On Resume**: SDK internally loads transcript via JSONL file
- **In Memory**: Active session maintains `Query` object that streams messages
- **Accumulation**: Token usage tracked across response (lines 320-325):
  ```javascript
  session.accumulatedUsage.inputTokens += message.usage.input_tokens;
  session.accumulatedUsage.outputTokens += message.usage.output_tokens;
  // etc.
  ```

**Streaming Architecture** (line 254-255):
- Prompt queue: `session.input` (Pushable stream)
- Response stream: `session.query.next()` async generator
- Multi-turn support: Queued prompts handled via `pendingMessages` map

**Files:**
- `/Users/kienduong/works/ai-agents/OpenACP/node_modules/@zed-industries/claude-agent-acp/dist/acp-agent.js` lines 218-537 (prompt loop)
- Lines 866-1090 (createSession & query configuration)

---

### 6. CONFIGURATION

**Settings Locations** (SettingsManager - lines 880-883):
1. User settings: `~/.claude/settings.json`
2. Project settings: `<cwd>/.claude/settings.json`
3. Local settings: `<cwd>/.claude/settings.local.json`
4. Enterprise managed settings (platform-specific)

**History-Related Options**:
- `resume?: string` - Session ID to resume (line 1083 in SDK)
- `forkSession?: boolean` - Fork on resume (line 884 in SDK)
- `sessionId?: string` - Custom session ID (line 1085-1087 in SDK)
- `maxTurns?: number` - Limit conversation turns (line 975-978 in SDK)

**Configuration Source**:
- File: `/Users/kienduong/works/ai-agents/OpenACP/node_modules/@zed-industries/claude-agent-acp/dist/settings.d.ts` lines 12-19
- Implementation: `/Users/kienduong/works/ai-agents/OpenACP/node_modules/@zed-industries/claude-agent-acp/dist/settings.js`

**Environment Variables**:
- `CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR` - Override config directory (line 11)
- `MAX_THINKING_TOKENS` - Thinking budget (line 925)
- `CLAUDE_CODE_EXECUTABLE` - Path to Claude Code binary (line 959-963)

---

### 7. KEY INSIGHTS

1. **Disk-Based Persistence**: Sessions are persisted as JSONL files managed by the Claude Agent SDK. The ACP package does NOT implement its own session storage.

2. **In-Memory State**: Only active/open sessions are kept in `this.sessions[sessionId]` dictionary. Closed sessions are deleted from memory but remain on disk.

3. **Transparent Resume**: When resuming, the SDK automatically detects and loads the JSONL transcript file by session ID. The ACP layer doesn't manually manage this.

4. **History Replay**: Explicit history replay only happens with `loadSession()` → `replaySessionHistory()`. This converts JSONL records to ACP notifications (lines 657-671).

5. **Multi-Turn Queueing**: The package implements a prompt queue (lines 235-247) to handle concurrent/queued prompts by mapping pending messages and resolving them in order.

6. **No Custom Persistence Layer Needed**: The Claude Agent SDK's `query()` function with `resume` option automatically handles session recovery from disk.

7. **Session Metadata**: Accessible via `listSessions()` which reads session summaries:
   - `sessionId`: UUID
   - `summary`: Auto-generated or custom title
   - `lastModified`: Timestamp (ms)
   - `cwd`: Working directory
   - `createdAt`: Creation timestamp
   - `customTitle`, `firstPrompt`, `gitBranch`, `tag`: Optional metadata

---

### Files Summary

| File | Purpose | Key Lines |
|------|---------|-----------|
| `acp-agent.d.ts` | Type definitions & interfaces | 21-38 (Session type), 154-164 (conversions) |
| `acp-agent.js` | Main ACP implementation | 146-193 (session lifecycle), 218-537 (prompt handling), 657-671 (history replay), 842-878 (createSession), 1007-1010 (query init) |
| `settings.d.ts` | Settings & configuration | 12-19 (ClaudeCodeSettings), 42-105 (SettingsManager) |
| `sdk.d.ts` | Claude Agent SDK types | 452 (getSessionMessages), 531 (listSessions), 1559 (query), 2130-2171 (SDKSessionInfo) |
| `index.js` | Entry point | 5-26 (CLI mode handling) |

content.content: Updated task #1

content.text:
Phát hiện rất quan trọng! `claude-agent-acp` đã có sẵn cơ chế persistence:

**Những gì đã có trong claude-agent-acp:**

1. **Session history lưu trên disk** — JSONL transcript files, managed bởi Claude Agent SDK
2. **Resume session** — `unstable_resumeSession(sessionId)` load lại session từ disk
3. **Fork session** — Tạo branch từ session cũ với history copy
4. **Load + replay history** — `loadSession()` replay toàn bộ conversation qua ACP notifications
5. **List sessions** — `listSessions()` trả metadata: sessionId, summary, lastModified, cwd...

**ACP capabilities đã advertise:**
```
sessionCapabilities: { fork: {}, list: {}, resume: {}, close: {} }
```

Vậy agent side đã hỗ trợ resume rồi. Vấn đề nằm ở **OpenACP core** — không persist mapping `session ↔ topic ↔ agentSessionId`.

Quay lại câu hỏi: **Khi restart, bạn muốn behavior nào?**

A. **Auto-resume** — Restart xong, tự động reconnect tất cả session cũ vào đúng topic, user chat tiếp bình thường (agent nhớ conversation history)
B. **Manual resume** — User vào topic cũ, gõ `/resume` để reconnect session đó
C. **Cả A và B** — Auto-resume các session active, cho phép manual resume các session đã close

message.content: C. để performance thì khi user chat thì mới resum hay tự động resum luôn? giải quyết bài toán có 100 topic chẳng hạn?

content.text:
Câu hỏi hay. Với 100 topics:

**Auto-resume tất cả khi startup:**
- Spawn 100 agent subprocess cùng lúc → tốn RAM, CPU, API rate limit
- Phần lớn topic có thể user không dùng lại → lãng phí

**Lazy resume (chat mới resume):**
- 0 subprocess khi startup → khởi động nhanh
- Chỉ spawn agent khi user thực sự gửi message vào topic
- Latency thêm ~2-3s cho message đầu tiên (spawn + ACP handshake + resume)

**Câu hỏi tiếp:** Với các session cũ (đã close hoặc lâu không dùng), bạn muốn xử lý thế nào?

A. **Tất cả topic đều có thể resume** — bất kể bao lâu, miễn transcript còn trên disk
B. **TTL** — Chỉ resume session trong vòng X ngày (ví dụ 7 ngày), topic cũ hơn thì archive/đóng
C. **Giới hạn số lượng** — Chỉ giữ N session gần nhất, cũ hơn thì cleanup

message.content: A. nếu không có thì spawn mới và tự động mapping nhỉ?

content.text:
Đúng, flow sẽ là:

1. User gửi message vào topic → OpenACP lookup mapping
2. **Có mapping + transcript còn trên disk** → Resume session cũ (giữ history)
3. **Có mapping nhưng transcript mất** → Spawn session mới, gắn vào topic cũ
4. **Không có mapping** (topic lạ/cũ trước khi có persistence) → Spawn session mới, tạo mapping mới

**Câu hỏi tiếp:** Về storage cho mapping, bạn muốn dùng gì?

A. **JSON file** — Đơn giản, `~/.openacp/sessions.json`, đủ cho use case hiện tại
B. **SQLite** — Query tốt hơn khi scale, nhưng thêm dependency
C. **Dùng luôn config.json** — Gộp vào config hiện có, không thêm file mới

message.content: A

content.text:
## 3 Approaches

### Approach A: Adapter-level persistence (Recommended)

Mỗi adapter tự quản lý mapping file riêng (`~/.openacp/sessions.json`). Telegram adapter ghi/đọc mapping `topicId ↔ { agentSessionId, agentName, workingDir, status }`.

- **Pros:** Đơn giản, không thay đổi core abstractions, mỗi adapter chỉ persist data nó cần
- **Cons:** Nếu thêm adapter khác (Discord) phải implement lại logic persist

### Approach B: Core-level session store

Thêm `SessionStore` interface vào core, `JsonFileSessionStore` là default implementation. Core tự persist tất cả session metadata, adapter chỉ cần lưu thêm platform-specific data (topicId).

- **Pros:** Reusable cho mọi adapter, logic tập trung
- **Cons:** Phức tạp hơn, core phải biết về platform-specific fields (topicId), hoặc dùng generic metadata map

### Approach C: Event-sourced

Ghi log mọi session event ra file, rebuild state từ log khi startup. Không cần maintain JSON snapshot.

- **Pros:** Không mất data, có audit trail
- **Cons:** Over-engineering cho bài toán này, startup chậm khi nhiều events

---

**Recommendation: Approach A** — Đơn giản nhất, đủ cho bài toán hiện tại. Nếu sau này cần thêm adapter thì refactor lên Approach B cũng không khó vì data model giống nhau.

Bạn chọn approach nào?

message.content: A, nhưng nếu thêm cac adapter như slack, discord thì nên handle thế nào?

content.text:
Tốt, câu hỏi đúng. Thực ra có thể **kết hợp A + B**:

- **Core** cung cấp `SessionStore` chung — persist phần **common**: `sessionId`, `agentSessionId`, `agentName`, `workingDir`, `status`, `createdAt`, `channelId`
- Mỗi adapter chỉ thêm **platform metadata** vào record đó: Telegram thêm `topicId`, Discord thêm `threadId/channelId`, Slack thêm `thread_ts`

Cụ thể `~/.openacp/sessions.json`:
```json
{
  "session-abc": {
    "agentSessionId": "uuid-from-claude",
    "agentName": "claude",
    "workingDir": "~/workspace/project",
    "channelId": "telegram",
    "status": "active",
    "createdAt": "2026-03-20T...",
    "platform": {
      "topicId": 123
    }
  }
}
```

- `SessionStore` nằm ở core — đọc/ghi file, CRUD operations
- Adapter gọi `store.save(sessionId, { ...commonData, platform: { topicId } })`
- Adapter gọi `store.findByPlatform("telegram", "topicId", 123)` để lookup

Như vậy thêm adapter mới chỉ cần define `platform` fields của nó, không cần implement lại storage logic.

Bạn đồng ý hướng này không?

content.text:
## Design Section 1: SessionStore (Core) **Interface đơn giản cho adapter sử dụng:** ```typescript interface SessionRecord

> { sessionId: string // OpenACP session ID agentSessionId: string // ID từ claude-agent-acp (dùng để resume) agentName: string workingDir: string channelId: string // "telegram" | "discord" | "slack" status: "active" | "finished" | "error" createdAt: string // ISO timestamp lastActiveAt: string // ISO timestamp — cập nhật mỗi lần có message name?: string // Session name (auto-generated) platform: P // Platform-specific data } interface SessionStore { save(record: SessionRecord): void get(sessionId: string): SessionRecord | undefined findByPlatform(channelId: string, key: string, value: unknown): SessionRecord | undefined list(channelId?: string): SessionRecord[] remove(sessionId: string): void } ``` **Implementation:** `JsonFileSessionStore` — đọc/ghi `~/.openacp/sessions.json`, cache in-memory, flush to disk on write. Phần này nhìn ổn không?

message.content: ổn đấy. có 1 câu hỏi: khi nó nặng lên thì xử lý thế nào?

content.text:
Vài cách handle khi file lớn:

**Write performance** — Không flush toàn bộ file mỗi lần write:
- Debounce write: gom nhiều thay đổi trong 1-2s rồi flush 1 lần
- In-memory là source of truth, disk là backup

**Read performance** — Chỉ đọc file 1 lần khi startup, sau đó dùng in-memory Map

**File size** — Giả sử mỗi record ~500 bytes:
- 100 sessions ≈ 50KB — không đáng lo
- 1,000 sessions ≈ 500KB — vẫn OK
- 10,000+ sessions — đây mới là vấn đề

**Khi thực sự nặng**, 2 lựa chọn:

A. **Auto-cleanup** — Xóa record có status `finished`/`error` sau N ngày (ví dụ 30 ngày). Giữ file nhỏ tự nhiên
B. **Migrate sang SQLite** — Interface `SessionStore` không đổi, chỉ swap implementation

Recommend: **Thêm auto-cleanup ngay từ đầu** (đơn giản, vài dòng code), ghi rõ trong design rằng `SessionStore` là interface — nếu cần scale thì swap sang SQLite mà adapter code không đổi.

Đồng ý hướng này không?

message.content: chuẩn luôn. nhưng cần thêm notice cho user biết là nên xoá các topic không cần đi không? hay tự động clean thôi

content.text:
Nên **tự động clean record** + **thông báo cho user về topic**:

- **Record trong sessions.json** — tự động xóa khi hết TTL, user không cần biết
- **Telegram topic** — không nên tự xóa vì user có thể muốn đọc lại history. Thay vào đó:
  - Khi cleanup record, gửi 1 message tổng hợp vào topic Notifications: "X sessions đã hết hạn, các topic sau không còn active: [list]. Bạn có thể xóa chúng nếu không cần."
  - Hoặc thêm command `/cleanup` để user chủ động xóa các topic đã expired

Tóm lại:
- **Data (record):** auto-clean, silent
- **UI (topic):** thông báo, user quyết định xóa

Đồng ý không?

content.text:
## Design Section 2: Lazy Resume Flow

Khi user gửi message vào topic:

```
User message → Adapter nhận message với topicId
  → store.findByPlatform("telegram", "topicId", topicId)
  → Có record?
    → Yes: session đang active trong memory?
      → Yes: forward message bình thường
      → No: spawn agent → resume(agentSessionId) → gắn vào topic → forward message
    → No: spawn session mới → tạo record mới với topicId → forward message
```

**Key points:**
- User không cảm nhận gì khác — gửi message, nhận reply
- Lần đầu sau restart chậm hơn ~2-3s (spawn + resume)
- Nếu resume fail (transcript mất) → spawn mới, update record

Section này ổn không?

content.text:
## Design Section 3: Record Lifecycle & Auto-cleanup

**Khi nào ghi record:**
- `save()` khi tạo session mới hoặc resume thành công
- Update `lastActiveAt` mỗi lần có message (debounce 2s)
- Update `status` khi session finish/error

**Auto-cleanup:**
- Chạy 1 lần khi startup
- Xóa record có `lastActiveAt` > 30 ngày (configurable)
- Chỉ xóa record, không xóa Telegram topic
- TODO: Thông báo user về topic expired (not implemented)

**Debounce write:**
- In-memory Map là source of truth
- Flush to disk sau 2s kể từ thay đổi cuối
- Force flush khi process shutdown (SIGTERM/SIGINT)

Section này ổn không?

**Core thay đổi:**
- `SessionManager` — inject `SessionStore`, gọi `save()`/`remove()` khi create/destroy session

**AgentInstance thay đổi:**
- Thêm static method `resume()` — giống `spawn()` nhưng gọi `unstable_resumeSession(agentSessionId)` thay vì `newSession()`

**Telegram Adapter thay đổi:**
- `sendMessage()` — khi nhận message từ topic chưa có session trong memory → trigger lazy resume
- Platform metadata type: `{ topicId: number }`

**Config thay đổi:**
- Thêm `sessionStore.ttlDays` vào config schema (default 30)

**File mới:**
- `src/core/session-store.ts` — `SessionStore` interface + `JsonFileSessionStore` implementation
